renderer_policy.data_evidence_default_rule: current evidence_figure templates are retained only when their renderer is R/ggplot2; a Python evidence template can re-enter only as a current audited template with documented advantage over the R/ggplot2 baseline
renderer_policy.python_evidence_retention_rule: not retained in the current pack without documented advantage proof and visual audit
renderer_policy.design_flow_renderer_rule: illustration_shell templates may use SVG, Python composition, or imagegen-assisted art direction because they do not act as statistical evidence authority

renderer_policy.data_evidence_default_rule: current evidence_figure templates are retained only when their renderer is R/ggplot2; a Python evidence template can re-enter only as a current audited template with documented advantage over the R/ggplot2 baseline
renderer_policy.python_evidence_retention_rule: not retained in the current pack without documented advantage proof and visual audit
renderer_policy.design_flow_renderer_rule: illustration_shell templates may use SVG, Python composition, or imagegen-assisted art direction because they do not act as statistical evidence authority

analysis_responsibility_policy.computed_in_template: Template renderer computes a bounded analysis workflow from declared raw input.
analysis_responsibility_policy.validated_summary_required: Template renderer accepts upstream validated analysis output only.
analysis_responsibility_policy.illustration_shell: Template is a non-statistical design or flow shell.
analysis_responsibility_policy.table_shell: Template accepts reviewed table values only.

rejected_patterns.reason: MAS evidence figures must keep the autonomous path smooth; default evidence routing uses R/ggplot2 and only routes Python when explicitly justified.
Nature-Skills remains a clean-room learning source, not a MAS runtime, selector, or publication authority.

source_refs.lesson: Start from a figure contract: core conclusion, evidence chain, panel hierarchy, backend-exclusive render/export, and final visual QA.
Check final-size lettering, line weight, reduced-size readability, vector line art, RGB color, and production-quality export before paper use.
Keep article-level visual grammar in one theme and guide system rather than per-template styling drift.
Select qualitative, sequential, and diverging palettes by data semantics and accessibility, not by plot-local taste.
Use perceptually uniform sequential scales for unsigned continuous evidence encodings.
Use fixed value-to-color mappings for comparable matrix heatmaps instead of per-figure color drift.
Treat multipanel figures as claim-led storyboards with shared legends and explicit hierarchy.
Survival figures need risk table, censor marks, event definition, and time scale reviewed together.

publication_polish_policy.residual_limit: This policy improves the default lower bound and agent hints; it does not authorize publication readiness, replace visual audit, or replace paper-local owner review.

policy.purpose: Page-level composition recipes for medical manuscript figures. They organize a small set of evidence primitives into claim-led figure pages instead of adding duplicate single-plot templates.
policy.learned_from: Yuan1z0825/nature-skills/skills/nature-figure page-level figure contract, common patterns, design theory, and QA workflow

policy.purpose: Page-level composition recipes for medical manuscript figures. They organize a small set of evidence primitives into claim-led figure pages instead of adding duplicate single-plot templates.
policy.learned_from: Yuan1z0825/nature-skills/skills/nature-figure page-level figure contract, common patterns, design theory, and QA workflow

recipes.title: Clinical Prediction Triptych
recipes.intent: Show discrimination, calibration, and clinical utility as one coherent model-performance figure page.
recipes.guide_strategy: shared_legend_or_direct_labels; no repeated bottom legends
recipes.label_strategy: directly label model curves when legend would crowd; keep thresholds near curves

recipes.title: Model Validation Dashboard
recipes.intent: Summarize predictive model robustness, calibration, explanation, complexity, subgroup behavior, and threshold governance without duplicating single-plot templates.
recipes.guide_strategy: reuse model/cohort palette roles; direct labels for feature lists when legends crowd
recipes.label_strategy: prioritize direct cohort, subgroup, and feature labels over repeated legends

recipes.title: Schematic-led Composite
recipes.intent: Start with a mechanism, workflow, or study-design schematic and attach the minimal quantitative panels needed to support the claim.
recipes.guide_strategy: use shared visual vocabulary in the schematic; quantitative panels keep article palette roles
recipes.label_strategy: panel labels and callouts explain flow; evidence panels use direct data labels only when needed

recipes.title: Image Plate plus Quantification
recipes.intent: Combine representative biomedical image panels with programmatic quantification and scale/channel labeling.
recipes.guide_strategy: keep image channels and quantitative palettes semantically separate
recipes.label_strategy: scale bars and channel labels on images; concise axes and direct group labels on quant panels

recipes.title: Asymmetric Genomics Figure
recipes.intent: Give molecular or multi-omic structure a dominant pattern panel and use smaller panels for consequence, enrichment, or clinical association.
recipes.guide_strategy: fixed matrix palette roles and shared annotation legends; avoid per-panel color drift
recipes.label_strategy: label only selected genes/pathways; group dense labels into annotation tracks

recipes.title: Single-cell or Spatial Atlas Storyboard
recipes.intent: Organize atlas-level state geometry, marker evidence, composition, trajectory, or spatial context into a readable multi-panel figure.
recipes.guide_strategy: shared cluster palette and direct labels for key states; separate continuous feature colorbars
recipes.label_strategy: label major states on the embedding; keep marker labels readable and sparse

composition_gallery_surface.storyboard_preview_policy: visual storyboards show page-level organization using current evidence primitive thumbnails and placeholders; they are not paper-local data results

items.title: Kaplan-Meier survival with risk table
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template is grouped Kaplan-Meier evidence; keep PDF reference id as external source id.

items.title: ROC curve with model comparison
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template names the binary ROC curve surface; roc_auc remains a source renderer id.

items.title: Cox/effect forest plot
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template generalizes Cox forest to effect forest while preserving the LidocaineQ source renderer.

items.title: Distribution comparison violin-box
items.replacement_reason: PDF reference id is preserved as LidocaineQ source id; MAS current canonical template replaces the retired duplicate alias.

items.title: Stacked proportion bars
items.replacement_reason: PDF reference id is preserved as LidocaineQ source id; MAS current canonical template replaces the retired duplicate alias.

items.title: Scatter correlation panel
items.replacement_reason: PDF reference id is preserved as LidocaineQ source id; MAS current canonical template replaces the retired duplicate alias.

items.title: Embedding scatter
items.replacement_reason: One LidocaineQ embedding reference covers MAS current PCA, t-SNE, and UMAP computed-workflow templates; do not add a duplicate external-id alias.

items.title: ComplexHeatmap annotated matrix
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template names the group-comparison heatmap surface; heatmap remains the external source renderer id.

items.title: Differential-expression volcano
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template names the omics volcano panel surface; volcano_deg remains the source renderer id.

items.title: Pathway enrichment dotplot
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template names the pathway enrichment dotplot surface; gsea_enrichment remains the source renderer id.

items.title: Mutation landscape oncoplot
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template names the genomic alteration landscape surface; oncoplot_mutation remains the source renderer id.

items.title: Ranked risk-score waterfall
items.replacement_reason: PDF reference id is preserved as LidocaineQ source id; MAS current canonical template replaces the retired duplicate alias.

items.title: Subtype transition alluvial
items.replacement_reason: PDF reference id is preserved as LidocaineQ source id; MAS current canonical template replaces the retired duplicate alias.

items.title: Radial immune/pathology profile
items.replacement_reason: PDF reference id is preserved as LidocaineQ source id; MAS current canonical template replaces the retired duplicate alias.

items.title: Baseline summary table
items.replacement_reason: MAS current canonical template keeps Table 1 semantics and uses a Gallery-only R preview for the LidocaineQ baseline table reference.

source_refs.lesson: Start from a figure contract: core conclusion, evidence chain, panel hierarchy, backend-exclusive render/export, and final visual QA.
Check final-size lettering, line weight, reduced-size readability, vector line art, RGB color, and production-quality export before paper use.
Keep article-level visual grammar in one theme and guide system rather than per-template styling drift.
Select qualitative, sequential, and diverging palettes by data semantics and accessibility, not by plot-local taste.
Use perceptually uniform sequential scales for unsigned continuous evidence encodings.
Use fixed value-to-color mappings for comparable matrix heatmaps instead of per-figure color drift.
Treat multipanel figures as claim-led storyboards with shared legends and explicit hierarchy.
Survival figures need risk table, censor marks, event definition, and time scale reviewed together.

publication_polish_policy.residual_limit: This policy improves the default lower bound and agent hints; it does not authorize publication readiness, replace visual audit, or replace paper-local owner review.

reference_learning_sources.lesson: Use consistent figure lettering, readable reduced-size labels, vector line art when possible, RGB color, and production-quality figure files.
Use a single theme system for titles, labels, fonts, backgrounds, gridlines, and legends so all evidence figures share one article-level visual grammar.
Nature Publishing Group inspired discrete palettes are mature ggplot2-compatible references for publication-style categorical roles.
HCL-based qualitative, sequential and diverging palettes are a stable basis for article-level semantic color roles.
Perceptually uniform and color-vision-friendly sequential palettes are preferred for continuous matrix and density-like encodings.
Matrix heatmaps need fixed value-to-color mapping rather than per-plot drift; shared sequential and diverging mappings preserve cross-figure comparability.
Figure work should start from core conclusion, evidence chain, panel hierarchy, backend-exclusive export, and final visual QA; MAS adapts this into a nonblocking R-first agent contract.
Scientific figure skills should be discoverable, task-scoped, and provenance-carrying instead of hidden inside a generic plotting prompt.
Reference-driven figure agents separate retrieval, planning, styling, visualization, and critique; MAS adapts this as refs-only quality-floor evidence.
Academic illustration generation benefits from multiple candidates and critic rounds before owner review rather than single-shot template reuse.
Reference matching needs an explicit preserve list so style transfer does not drift away from claim, evidence, labels, or source constraints.
Small hard rules for vector export, dimensions, color, typography, and parameter blocks raise the plotting floor without constraining the figure concept.
Graphical abstract skills are easier to review and revise when the visual source remains editable instead of only producing a flattened image.
A simple three-panel flow remains useful as a low-risk default only when it is treated as a brief-driven composition skeleton, not a fixed final visual system.
Prompt collections are most useful when converted into explicit brief, preserve-list, candidate, and critic gates rather than copied as prose prompts.
Graphical abstracts vary by field and paper intent; a renderer should preserve AI choice of composition while enforcing readable layout and evidence constraints.

rejected_patterns.reason: MAS evidence figures must keep the autonomous path smooth; default evidence routing uses R/ggplot2 and only routes Python when explicitly justified.
Nature-Skills remains a clean-room learning source, not a MAS runtime, selector, or publication authority.

Use perceptually uniform sequential scales for unsigned continuous evidence encodings.
Use fixed value-to-color mappings for comparable matrix heatmaps instead of per-figure color drift.
Treat multipanel figures as claim-led storyboards with shared legends and explicit hierarchy.
Survival figures need risk table, censor marks, event definition, and time scale reviewed together.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may replace sankey with transition matrix when there are too many flows.

recommended_next_actions: Use this template as a lower-bound starting point; AI may freely alter structure, layout, labels, scale, and composition for the paper-specific claim.
Render-inspect-revise before any paper-facing use; do not accept the synthetic Gallery preview as final artwork.

canonical_category_ontology.title: Machine Learning Explanation and Causal Inference

canonical_family_ontology.title: CONSORT Trial Flow
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show randomized trial participant screening, allocation, follow-up, and analysis populations.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Observational Cohort Flow
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show source population, eligibility filters, exclusions, final analytic cohorts, and endpoint availability.

canonical_family_ontology.title: PRISMA Review Flow
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show systematic review identification, screening, eligibility, inclusion, automation exclusions, and reasons for exclusion.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Diagnostic Accuracy Flow
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show participant flow through index test, reference standard, exclusions, indeterminate results, and analysis set.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Eligibility Waterfall
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show stepwise sample attrition from source population to final analytic set.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Study Timeline
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show enrollment, exposure, follow-up, assessment windows, and analysis milestones.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Table 1 Baseline Characteristics
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Summarize baseline variables by cohort, group, or treatment arm with appropriate summaries.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Distribution Comparison
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Compare continuous or ordinal variable distributions across groups.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Paired or Within-subject Change
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show paired baseline-to-follow-up changes or matched observations.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Missingness Map
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show missing data patterns across variables, cohorts, or timepoints.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Correlation Matrix
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show relationships among variables, biomarkers, or model features.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Correlation Scatter
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show pairwise continuous association with optional group coloring, trend line, and statistical annotation.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Composition Profile
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show categorical composition such as sex, race, cell type, event type, or response class across groups.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Profile Radar
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show compact group profiles across a small number of comparable scaled dimensions.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Effect Estimate Forest
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show point estimates and intervals for effects, odds ratios, hazard ratios, risk differences, or coefficients.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Subgroup and Interaction Forest
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show treatment or exposure effect heterogeneity across clinically meaningful subgroups.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Dose-response or Spline Curve
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show nonlinear association between continuous exposure and outcome.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Coefficient Path
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show variable coefficient evolution across regularization strength or model sequence.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Sensitivity Analysis Panel
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show robustness of estimates across alternative definitions, adjustments, exclusions, or assumptions.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Kaplan-Meier Curve with Risk Table
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show survival probability over time with number at risk and censoring information.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Cumulative Incidence with Competing Risks
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show event incidence over time when competing events matter.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Hazard over Time
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show time-varying hazard or hazard ratio patterns.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Landmark Survival Panel
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show survival or prediction performance from a clinically meaningful landmark time.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Restricted Mean Survival Time Difference
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show absolute survival-time differences over clinically relevant horizons.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Discrimination Curve
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show model discrimination using ROC, PR, time-dependent ROC, AUC, or C-statistic curves.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Calibration Panel
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show agreement between predicted and observed risk with uncertainty and calibration summaries.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Decision Curve Analysis
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show net benefit or clinical utility across threshold probabilities.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Confusion Matrix
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show classification results by predicted and observed classes.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Risk Stratification
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show risk group separation, observed event rates, or monotonic risk layering.

canonical_family_ontology.title: External Validation Performance
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show model performance across cohorts, centers, countries, or temporal splits.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Nomogram or Scoring Rule
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show clinical score points, total score, and risk translation.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Model Complexity and Operating Point Audit
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show model complexity, threshold operating characteristics, and audit diagnostics that explain whether a prediction model is usable without overfitting or unsafe threshold behavior.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Swimmer Treatment Course
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show individual patient treatment duration, response, progression, and follow-up events.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Waterfall Response
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show best percentage change in lesion burden or biomarker response by patient.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Spider Response
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show longitudinal tumor or biomarker change for individual patients.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Adverse Event Profile
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show adverse event frequency and severity by arm or subgroup.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Dose Escalation Schema
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show dose levels, cohorts, DLTs, and escalation/de-escalation decisions.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Meta-analysis Forest
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show study-specific and pooled effect estimates with weights and intervals.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Funnel Plot and Bias Diagnostics
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show study-size or precision relationship to effect estimates for small-study effect assessment.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Leave-one-out Influence
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show effect of removing each study or cohort on pooled estimate.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Baujat Heterogeneity
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show study contribution to heterogeneity and pooled effect.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Network Meta-analysis Map
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show treatment comparison network and evidence density.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Evidence Grade Map
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show evidence certainty, risk of bias, and recommendation strength across outcomes.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Volcano or MA Plot
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show differential signal magnitude and significance for genes, proteins, metabolites, or features.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Annotated Heatmap
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show matrix patterns with sample, feature, group, or pathway annotations.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Dimension Reduction Scatter
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show PCA, UMAP, t-SNE, PHATE, or diffusion-map embedding colored by group or continuous signal.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Pathway Enrichment Dotplot
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show pathway or gene-set enrichment magnitude, significance, and gene ratio.

canonical_family_ontology.title: GSEA Running Score
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show gene-set enrichment running score, hits, and ranked-list metric.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Genomic Alteration Landscape
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show mutation, CNV, fusion, or alteration landscape across samples and genes.

canonical_family_ontology.title: CNV or Genome-wide Summary
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show genomic copy-number or chromosome-level alteration patterns.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Genomic Alteration Consequence Summary
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show downstream molecular, pathway, or clinical consequences of genomic alterations without duplicating the primary alteration landscape.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Single-cell Embedding
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show single-cell clusters, cell types, samples, or conditions in low-dimensional embedding.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Feature Expression Map
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show marker, gene, module score, or continuous feature on an embedding or spatial map.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Marker Dotplot
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show marker expression prevalence and magnitude across cell types or clusters.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Cell Composition
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show cell-type abundance or proportions across samples, groups, tissues, or regions.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Trajectory and Pseudotime
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show inferred cellular trajectory, pseudotime, lineage branches, or progression states.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Spatial Feature Map
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show spatial localization of expression, cell types, niches, or histology-linked signals.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Cell-cell Interaction
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show inferred ligand-receptor, neighborhood, or interaction strengths between cell types.

canonical_family_ontology.title: SHAP or Feature Importance Summary
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show global model explanation, feature importance, and directionality.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Local Model Explanation
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show patient-level or sample-level explanation of model prediction.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Feature Response Curve
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show marginal or conditional model response to feature changes.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Causal DAG
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show assumed causal structure, confounding, mediation, or adjustment set.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Propensity Balance Love Plot
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show covariate balance before and after matching, weighting, or adjustment.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Decision Threshold Governance
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show threshold choice, utility tradeoffs, subgroup consequences, or operating points.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Longitudinal Spaghetti Trajectory
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show repeated measurement trajectories by patient, group, or subgroup.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Event Timeline
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show clinical events, exposures, outcomes, or interventions over patient time.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Alluvial Transition
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show categorical state transitions across time, treatment lines, or care pathways.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Recurrent Event Plot
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show repeated events over follow-up by subject or group.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Laboratory or Biomarker Trajectory
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show longitudinal biomarker, lab, or physiological marker changes.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Multipanel Storyboard
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Compose multiple evidence panels into a coherent manuscript figure narrative.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Graphical Abstract
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Summarize study question, data, method, and main finding in a visual overview.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Mechanism Schematic
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show biological, clinical, or algorithmic mechanism supporting the manuscript narrative.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Source Data Companion
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Show compact source-data, reproducibility, or evidence trace companion panel.

canonical_family_ontology.title: Supplement Dense Panel
canonical_family_ontology.intent: Pack many secondary checks into a readable supplement figure.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may switch to density plot if continuous risk distribution is the main claim.

templates.migration_reason: Risk-group summary tables and bars are one risk-layering display intent.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may switch between ROC and PR when class imbalance makes PR more informative.

templates.migration_reason: Binary ROC is a distinct prediction-performance claim and remains a current R/ggplot2 template.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI should include support/rug or warning when response is extrapolated beyond observed data.

templates.migration_reason: Dependence plots answer a feature-response explanation claim and remain separate from global ranking.

starter_recipe_titles: SHAP or Feature Importance Summary starter
ai_adaptation_notes: AI may replace beeswarm with grouped importance when many points or features obscure interpretation.

templates.migration_reason: SHAP bar and multicohort importance are modes of the global explanation summary seed.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may redact patient identifiers and must avoid implying causal effect from predictive contribution.

templates.migration_reason: Force-like local explanation is a local-explanation mode, not a global SHAP template.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may switch between ROC and PR when class imbalance makes PR more informative.

templates.migration_reason: Multi-horizon and landmark discrimination panels are presets of the time-dependent discrimination recipe; calibration is kept as its own current template.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI must keep treat-all/treat-none baselines clear and may restrict threshold range to clinically relevant values.

templates.migration_reason: Threshold governance is a decision-curve mode; the current template exposes the clinical utility grammar directly.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may add risk distribution rug or histogram to expose unsupported ranges.

templates.migration_reason: Survival calibration is a distinct model-validation claim and remains separate from discrimination curves.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may facet by cohort or overlay centroids when overplotting obscures groups.

templates.migration_reason: t-SNE is a fixed classic analysis recipe and remains separate from PCA and UMAP.

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may facet by cohort or overlay centroids when overplotting obscures groups.

templates.migration_reason: PHATE and diffusion-map templates are retired as separate current templates; UMAP carries the nonlinear embedding scatter lower-bound seed.

templates.canonical_family_wording: Waterfall Response (Clinical Trial Response and Safety): ranked_patient_response_waterfall

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may add threshold lines and mutation annotations when they support the response claim.

templates.migration_reason: Patient-level response waterfall is a distinct oncology/trial response grammar and should be callable as its own lower-bound seed.

reporting_flow_gallery_templates.canonical_family_wording: Cohort Flow (Publication Shells and Tables): consort_strobe_participant_flow

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may use tree, sankey, or waterfall layout when cohort construction is easier to read that way.
AI may redesign the flow diagram layout for complex arms or adaptive trials, but must keep counts and exclusion reasons auditable.
AI may collapse low-value boxes for space only when all PRISMA counts remain traceable in caption or supplement.
AI may use swimlane layout when index-test and reference-standard pathways diverge.
AI may switch to flow diagram when exclusions branch rather than occur sequentially.

reporting_flow_gallery_templates.migration_reason: Cohort-flow variants are unified into one structured reporting-flow template driven by participant accounting and exclusion counts.

language_package_requirements.role: ggconsort cohort data manipulation and plotting helpers

language_package_requirements.role: mature CONSORT/STROBE-style cohort participant flow grammar

language_package_requirements.name: grid
language_package_requirements.role: grob layout inspection for sidecars

design_gallery_templates.canonical_family_wording: Submission Graphical Abstract (Publication Shells and Tables): submission_graphical_abstract_shell

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may use generated illustration only if it carries no unsupported scientific evidence mark.
AI may redraw for clarity but must not invent causal assumptions not stated in protocol.

design_gallery_templates.migration_reason: Submission graphical abstracts remain an expressive composition shell, not a statistical evidence template.

table_preview_gallery_templates.canonical_family_wording: Table 1 Baseline Characteristics (Publication Shells and Tables): baseline_characteristics_table_shell

ai_adaptation_notes: AI may choose table, dot summary, or hybrid table-figure when visual comparison is clearer.

table_preview_gallery_templates.migration_reason: Table numbering and downstream table summaries are paper-local surfaces; the default pack exposes one table-shell seed.

migration_index.migration_reason: Alluvial state transitions are a common patient/pathway composition grammar and should not be represented as generic stacked bars.
The old combined calibration plus decision panel is a composition preset; current templates keep calibration and utility as separate editable seeds.
Marker dotplots share dot marks with enrichment plots but carry cell-type marker-expression semantics.
CNV recurrence has a distinct genome-position and recurrence-frequency grammar.
Coefficient paths represent a distinct ordered-model-step analysis recipe.
Cohort-flow variants are unified into one structured reporting-flow template driven by participant accounting and exclusion counts.
Student-curated stacked composition bars provide a distinct population/composition grammar and avoid overloading generic bar charts.
Confusion matrices use heatmap marks but have fixed classification semantics and normalization constraints.
Pairwise association scatter is visually and semantically distinct from correlation heatmaps and effect forest plots.
Stratified cumulative-incidence variants are modes of the same cumulative-incidence grammar.
Clinical impact is a decision-curve presentation mode, not a separate default evidence template.
Student-curated distribution template fills a distinct common medical-paper distribution comparison intent; it is not a heatmap/ROC/KM duplicate.
Subgroup, multivariable, compact, heterogeneity and interaction variants share the same effect-estimate grammar.
Generalizability and transportability panels carry a distinct external-validity claim.
Multiomic and pathway-integrated consequence panels are composition presets of the consequence summary intent.
Oncoplot and genomic governance variants are retired as separate defaults and map to the genomic landscape seed.
Generic heatmap variants differ mainly by input source and annotation; they now share one current matrix heatmap template with a unified palette.
Kaplan-Meier survival curves carry a distinct time-to-event claim.
Model audit templates carry diagnostic semantics rather than primary result-display semantics.
Volcano plots have distinct threshold, label and effect-significance grammar.
Pathway enrichment dotplots are a common fixed analysis recipe.
PCA is a fixed classic analysis recipe and remains separate from nonlinear embeddings.
Precision-recall curves answer an imbalanced-class performance claim and remain separate from ROC.
Radar profile is retained as a compact profile overview seed when axes are few and comparable; AI may replace it when bars or dotplots are clearer.
Risk-group summary tables and bars are one risk-layering display intent.
Binary ROC is a distinct prediction-performance claim and remains a current R/ggplot2 template.
Dependence plots answer a feature-response explanation claim and remain separate from global ranking.
SHAP bar and multicohort importance are modes of the global explanation summary seed.
Force-like local explanation is a local-explanation mode, not a global SHAP template.
Submission graphical abstracts remain an expressive composition shell, not a statistical evidence template.
Table numbering and downstream table summaries are paper-local surfaces; the default pack exposes one table-shell seed.
Multi-horizon and landmark discrimination panels are presets of the time-dependent discrimination recipe; calibration is kept as its own current template.
Threshold governance is a decision-curve mode; the current template exposes the clinical utility grammar directly.
Survival calibration is a distinct model-validation claim and remains separate from discrimination curves.
t-SNE is a fixed classic analysis recipe and remains separate from PCA and UMAP.
PHATE and diffusion-map templates are retired as separate current templates; UMAP carries the nonlinear embedding scatter lower-bound seed.
Patient-level response waterfall is a distinct oncology/trial response grammar and should be callable as its own lower-bound seed.
